title: Kabeer Biswas’s concierge startup 'M' raises Rs 102 crore from Peak XV, Blume, Cred
description: Kabeer Biswas, Dunzo cofounder, has launched a new AI concierge startup named M. The company secured Rs 102 crore in funding from Peak XV Partners, Blume Ventures, and Cred. M aims to automate household tasks using artificial intelligence. This venture follows Biswas's exit from Flipkart.
content:

title: India's central bank in talks with global regulators, banks to review Mythos risks, sources say
description: India’s central bank is in talks with global regulators, Indian lenders and government officials to understand the potential risks posed by Anthropic’s new artificial intelligence ​model Mythos, three sources said.
content:
MUMBAI, April 22 (Reuters) - India’s central bank is in talks with global regulators, Indian lenders and government officials to understand the potential risks posed by Anthropic’s new artificial intelligence ​model Mythos, three sources said.

title: Finally, an answer: how much is a pint of Guinness in every British pub?
description: A pair of tech engineers use artificial intelligence to find the UK’s ‘Guinness Equator’ and reveal where a pint of the black stuff costs £7.61
content:
Some technology evangelists say artificial intelligence will solve the great philosophical questions of our age. One AI engineer has used it to solve a much more important question: where in Britain can you get a cheap pint of Guinness?

title: Nearly 70 pc of new green jobs need tech skills; huge vacancy for skilled professionals

content:

title: Tesla Earnings: Cooling AI Hype Makes Investors Yawn
description: Tesla Inc. investors are in for a rare treat Wednesday afternoon: a blowout earnings report. The trouble is the actual numbers are likely to get overlooked as Wall Street seeks evidence that Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence and robotics ventures justify the stock’s sky-high valuation.

title: HCL Tech flags weak Q4, bets big on AI to drive next phase of growth
description: HCL Technologies faced a slower fourth quarter than anticipated. Weakness in its software business and certain service areas impacted results. However, the company sees significant future growth from Artificial Intelligence. New AI services are already generating substantial revenue. HCL Technologies expects AI to drive future business expansion and value creation.
